arrival.say: You there! Off the ferry! Don't waste what light's left, lad.
That lamp of yours is dead, isn't it.

look.text: Someone left a coil of tarred line by the piling.

take.text: You shoulder the coil of line.

look.text: A boathook rests across the rail, waiting for a boat.

take.text: You lift the boathook off the rail.

look.text: Empty packing crates, stacked chest-high. The lens crate isn't among them.

use.text: You shift a few. All empty, all splinters, no lens. Whatever came in these has long since gone up the tower.

look.text: The keeper's skiff, tied off and half-bailed. It won't be rowing anywhere heavy.

use.text: It's half full of harbour already. Row out in that and you'd be dredging the bottom the hard way — from underneath.

itemUse.text: No need to put to sea — the crate's close enough to reach from the pier. Cast from solid ground, not a leaky boat.

look.text: Past the skiff, straight down. Something pale catches the last light — the crate.
Cold, dark water, patient as ever.

itemUse.text: You swing the grapple out past the skiff and drag the bottom. On the third pass it bites — up comes the crate, and inside, the lens.
You'd be dragging blind. Better find out where to cast first.
Just line, no bite. It wants something with teeth on the end.
Throw the boathook in bare and the harbour keeps it. It needs a line.

look.text: The lighthouse keeper. Old rope for arms, weather for a face.

itemUse.speaker: target
itemUse.text: "Don't show it to me, lad — up the stairs with it! The housing's open."

itemUse.speaker: target
itemUse.text: "A fine rig. My dredging days are done, though — you swing it."

itemUse.speaker: target
itemUse.text: "Good line. But line alone won't grip a sunken crate — put some teeth on the end of it, lad."

itemUse.speaker: target
itemUse.text: "The old boathook! Lash it to a length of line and you'd have a proper grapple. I'll not do the knots for you, mind."

exits.name: lighthouse door
exits.look: An iron-banded door at the lighthouse's foot, standing ajar.
